expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: All three tiers present with proper markdown table headers and a Context section with Goal, Type, Domain

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories (e.g., npm, GitHub, Google Scholar, StackOverflow)
expectations.evidence: Uses GitHub, npm, Google Scholar, Google, StackOverflow, Semantic Scholar, YouTube, Hacker News

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as stars:>100, language:typescript, or site: filters
expectations.evidence: Includes language:typescript, stars:>100, site:blog.*, keywords:crdt, votes:5, and date filters

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 focuses on direct CRDT framework searches; Tier 2 adds StackOverflow and HN for practitioner input; Tier 3 uses Semantic Scholar and YouTube

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: All 11 rows have an Acceptance column with specific minimum-count thresholds

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: Runtime Recovery section contains 4 bullet points with concrete fallback strategies

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
expectations.evidence: Grading Summary table lists thresholds for Tier 1 (3/4), Tier 2 (2/4), Tier 3 (1/3), and Overall (6/11)

expectations.text: Queries specifically reference CRDT, operational transform, or offline-sync terminology
expectations.evidence: Multiple queries reference CRDT, conflict-free replicated data types, offline-first, and event sourcing

expectations.text: At least one query targets latency or performance benchmark data
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 row 3 targets Google Scholar for CRDT synchronization benchmarks; Tier 3 row 3 seeks latency numbers

expectations.text: Matrix covers at least 3 of the named frameworks (Automerge, Yjs, PowerSync, ElectricSQL)
expectations.evidence: Automerge, Yjs, PowerSync, and ElectricSQL are all referenced across Tier 1 and Tier 2 queries
